page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-384 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the c4d server family. It features 384 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 1488.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 3.88 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include integrated local storage or graphics processing units (GPUs), and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Operating with a high density of dedicated vCPUs, the instance is designed for massive parallel processing workloads. While benchmark data is not available to quantify specific execution speeds, the hardware profile indicates a balanced resource allocation between compute and memory. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of this instance allows organizations to consolidate large-scale workloads onto a single virtual machine, reducing the overhead associated with managing multiple smaller nodes.

The c4d-standard-384 is suited for highly threaded, resource-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include large-scale web serving, enterprise application hosting, batch processing, and distributed data processing tasks that require substantial memory capacity alongside high-core-count parallel execution without requiring local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-384 is a high-capacity virtual server belonging to the c4d family, built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. It is equipped with 384 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of approximately 3.88 GB of memory per core. This instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Designed for high-density compute tasks, the c4d-standard-384 offers a qualitative advantage for resource consolidation, allowing organizations to run massive parallel workloads on a single instance. It is highly suited for enterprise applications, large-scale web serving, batch processing, and distributed data processing workloads that demand significant CPU and memory resources without the need for specialized hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-384 is an x86_64 server featuring 384 dedicated vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM, designed for high-density parallel processing and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-standard-384 offers 384 dedicated vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM on x86_64 architecture for large-scale enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density x86_64 virtual server featuring 384 dedicated cores and balanced memory for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 1488.0 GB of system memory with 3.88 GB per core
No integrated local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for large-scale enterprise applications and batch processing
